meta.title: Eva-CLI | Multi-Agent Runtime
meta.description: Eva-CLI is an open-source CLI architecture for hot-reloadable multi-Agent runtimes, combining a Rust core, Lua capability layer, Topic EventBus, controlled memory and knowledge services, and dynamic adapters.

home.heroTagline: A controlled runtime for multi-Agent automation
home.heroLead: Eva-CLI uses Rust for reliable runtime boundaries and Lua for hot-reloadable Agent behavior, then connects external Agents, MCP servers, skills, memory, knowledge bases, and hardware through a unified Topic EventBus.

runtimeSteps.label: Rust Core
runtimeSteps.description: Owns policy, memory, recovery, and process boundaries.

runtimeSteps.label: Lua Agents
runtimeSteps.description: Hot-reloads behavior while keeping capability access explicit.

runtimeSteps.label: Adapters
runtimeSteps.description: Routes CLI, MCP, HTTP, skills, and hardware through permissions.

home.runtimePanelNote: Architecture specs are organized for implementation planning and review.
home.progressTitle: From architecture scaffold to runnable Runtime
home.progressCurrentTitle: Workspace, configuration tree, and Agent layout are in place
home.progressCurrentBody: The repository now has a compileable Rust workspace, configuration tree, JSON Schemas, sample Agents, Adapters, policies, routes, and a flat one-Agent-one-directory layout. Next practical work is configuration validation, Agent discovery, and a minimum runtime loop.
progressNextItems: Implement eva config validate / inspect / dump-effective.
Wire config/agents/<agent-id>/ into Agent discovery and registration.
Prove the minimum Ingress -> EventBus -> Scheduler -> Lua Agent -> Rust Tool loop.
home.featuredBlogTitle: Latest design notes from the blog
home.featuredBlogBody: Follow the implementation thinking behind Eva-CLI: runtime boundaries, LuaAgent tooling, product direction, and controlled automation design.

progressStages.label: Architecture, module partitioning, and configuration
progressStages.description: System boundaries, runtime model, adapters, memory, discovery, hardware, recovery, website, docs, configuration, and module partitioning are in place.

progressStages.label: Workspace scaffold and configuration samples
progressStages.description: The Rust workspace, crate boundaries, root CLI shim, configuration tree, schemas, sample Agents, and flat Agent layout are now in place.

progressStages.label: Configuration validation and discovery loop
progressStages.description: Implement configuration loading, schema validation, policy validation, Agent/Adapter discovery, and inspectable effective config.

progressStages.label: Runtime loop and module implementation
progressStages.description: Prove Ingress -> EventBus -> Scheduler -> Lua Agent -> Rust Tool, then fill in modules one slice at a time.

feedback.title: Feedback and suggestions
feedback.body: Send documentation, architecture, or use-case feedback by email. The repository is still in the design-spec stage, so clear constraints and missing contracts are especially useful.
feedback.subjectPlaceholder: For example: add a quick start example
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Describe the suggestion, problem, or missing content.
feedback.note: Submitting opens your local email client and sends the message to iyetmos@gmail.com.
